title: Fix save/load diagram persistence and restrict icon drag to image only

requirements.summary: Fix the save diagram functionality so that saved diagrams immediately appear in DiagramSidebar and LoadDiagramDialog without requiring a page reload
acceptanceCriteria: After saving a diagram via SaveDiagramDialog, the diagram appears in DiagramSidebar and LoadDiagramDialog immediately without a page reload.
Loading a saved diagram from the sidebar/dialog restores all icons, connections, drawings, lines, text labels, and images correctly.
The useSaveDiagramState mutation correctly invalidates the allDiagrams and diagramState caches on success so the list refreshes.
No data is lost between save and load operations.

file_operations.description: Update the mutation's onSuccess callback to invalidate both the 'allDiagrams' query key (used by DiagramSidebar and LoadDiagramDialog) and the 'diagramState' query key. Ensure queryClient.invalidateQueries is called with both keys to trigger immediate refetch of the diagram list.

After the save mutation succeeds, ensure the dialog closes and any local UI state is updated to reflect the newly saved diagram. Verify that the mutation's onSuccess handler properly triggers the sidebar and load dialog to refresh their lists.

Ensure the useGetAllDiagrams hook is properly configured to refetch when its query is invalidated. Verify that the component displays the updated list immediately after a save operation without requiring manual refresh or page reload.

Ensure the useGetAllDiagrams hook is properly configured to refetch when its query is invalidated. Verify that the dialog displays the updated list immediately after a save operation, showing the newly saved diagram in the list.

requirements.summary: Restrict drag-and-drop behavior on toolbar icon buttons so that only the icon image element is draggable, not the text label beneath it
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a toolbar icon button initiates the drag using only the icon image as the drag source/ghost, not the label text.
The label text beneath the icon is not draggable and does not appear in the drag ghost image.
Dropping the dragged icon onto the canvas places the correct icon type at the drop position.
All toolbar icon buttons exhibit this behavior consistently, not just the third button in div[7].

file_operations.description: Refactor the icon button rendering so that the draggable attribute and onDragStart handler are applied only to the element, not to the parent button or label text. Wrap the image in a container div if needed, and ensure the text label (beneath the icon) has no drag handlers. Verify that the drag ghost shows only the icon image, and that all toolbar icon buttons behave consistently.
